diffs.patch:
Index: content/docs/(root)/index.mdx
===================================================================
--- content/docs/(root)/index.mdx	
+++ content/docs/(root)/index.mdx	
@@ -1,62 +1,63 @@
 ---
 title: Introduction
 description: A shadcn registry by Pranjal Mandavkar, shipping polished, ready-to-install components.
 ---
 
 **prsui** is a [shadcn registry](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/registry) by [Pranjal Mandavkar](https://x.com/pr5dev) — a curated collection of polished, ready-to-install components you can drop into any React project.
 
 ## Add the Registry
 
 First, add the prsui registry to your project:
 
 <CodeBlockCommand
   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest registry add @prsui=https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/{name}.json"
   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest registry add @prsui=https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/{name}.json"
   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest registry add @prsui=https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/{name}.json"
   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest registry add @prsui=https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/{name}.json"
 />
 
 ## Components
 
 The registry currently ships:
 
 - **[Smooth Input](/docs/components/smooth-input)** - A smooth input component with a caret animation.
+- **[Mouse Follow](/docs/components/mouse-follow)** - Animated cursor-following component with spring physics and opacity transitions.
 
 ## FAQ
 
 <Accordion type="multiple">
 
 <AccordionItem value="faq-1">
 <AccordionTrigger>
 Why copy/paste instead of npm packages?
 </AccordionTrigger>
 <AccordionContent>
 
 The shadcn philosophy gives you **ownership** of the code. You can customize components to your exact needs without fighting library constraints.
 
 </AccordionContent>
 </AccordionItem>
 
 <AccordionItem value="faq-2">
 <AccordionTrigger>
 Which frameworks are supported?
 </AccordionTrigger>
 <AccordionContent>
 
 Any React framework: Next.js, Remix, Astro, Gatsby, Vite, etc.
 
 </AccordionContent>
 </AccordionItem>
 
 <AccordionItem value="faq-3">
 <AccordionTrigger>
 Can I request a component?
 </AccordionTrigger>
 <AccordionContent>
 
 Reach out to [Pranjal Mandavkar](https://x.com/pr5dev) with suggestions — more components are added over time.
 
 </AccordionContent>
 </AccordionItem>
 
 </Accordion>

Index: content/docs/components/mouse-follow.mdx =================================================================== --- content/docs/components/mouse-follow.mdx +++ content/docs/components/mouse-follow.mdx @@ -0,0 +1,64 @@ +--- +title: Mouse Follow +description: Animated cursor-following component with spring physics and opacity transitions. +--- + +import { MouseFollowPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/mouse-follow-demo"; + + + + + +## Installation + +If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command: + + + +Otherwise, use the full registry URL: + + + +## Usage + +After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder: + +```tsx +"use client"; + +import { SimpleMouseFollow, SpringMouseFollow } from "./mouse-follow"; + +export const MouseFollowPreview = () => ( +
+ + +
+); +``` + +## Exports + +| Export | Description | +| --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | +| `SimpleMouseFollow` | Basic mouse-following dot with opacity fade in/out on pointer enter/leave. | +| `SpringMouseFollow` | Spring-physics mouse-following dot with scale and opacity spring animation. | + +## Spring Config + +The `SpringMouseFollow` uses the following spring configuration: + +| Property | Value | Description | +| ---------- | ----- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | +| `mass` | `0.1` | Lower mass = snappier motion; higher mass = lethargic. | +| `damping` | `10` | Controls bounce — higher damping = less bounce. | +| `stiffness`| `131` | Like a rubber band — higher stiffness = faster return. |

Index: registry/new-york/mouse-follow-demo.tsx =================================================================== --- registry/new-york/mouse-follow-demo.tsx +++ registry/new-york/mouse-follow-demo.tsx @@ -0,0 +1,12 @@ +"use client"; + +import { SimpleMouseFollow, SpringMouseFollow } from "./mouse-follow"; + +export const MouseFollowPreview = () => ( +

input.prompt:
Explore the project at /home/himmel/personal/prsui to understand its structure. I need to know:

1. How is the registry organized? Look at `registry/` directory structure.
2. How are components documented? Look for docs files, especially how existing components like "marquee" or "globe" or any other component have their docs structured.
3. How are demos set up? Find demo files for existing components.
4. Is there a `registry.json` or similar file that registers components?
5. Look at the content directory - `content/docs/` or similar for MDX files.

Return:
- The directory structure of `registry/`
- A sample of how a component's docs page looks (full content of one component's MDX file)
- A sample of how a component's demo is structured
- The registry.json or equivalent file content (at least the first component entry)
- Any configuration files that list/register components

Be thorough - check for `content/`, `docs/`, `src/` directories. This is a shadcn-style registry project.

metadata.preview:
---
title: Typewriter
description: Animated typewriter effect with support for multiple phrases, looping, and a customizable cursor.
---

import { TypewriterPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/typewriter-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="typewriter">
  <TypewriterPreview />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/typewriter"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/typewriter"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/typewriter"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/typewriter"

display.text:
--- title: Typewriter description: Animated typewriter effect with support for multiple phrases, looping, and a customizable cursor. --- import { TypewriterPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/typewriter-demo"; ## Installation If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command: Otherwise, use the full registry URL: ## Usage After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder: ```tsx "use client"; import Typewriter from "./typewriter"; export const TypewriterPreview = () => (

To seek the truth we must

); ``` ## Props | Prop | Type | Default | Description | | ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | | `text` | `string \| string[]` | - | Text to type. Pass an array to cycle through phrases. | | `as` | `ElementType` | `"div"` | Element or component to render as. | | `speed` | `number` | `50` | Typing speed in ms per character. | | `initialDelay` | `number` | `0` | Delay before typing starts in ms. | | `waitTime` | `number` | `2000` | Pause before deleting or moving to next phrase in ms. | | `deleteSpeed` | `number` | `30` | Deleting speed in ms per character. | | `loop` | `boolean` | `true` | Whether to loop through the phrases. | | `className` | `string` | - | Classes applied to the wrapper element. | | `showCursor` | `boolean` | `true` | Whether to show the cursor. | | `hideCursorOnType` | `boolean` | `false` | Hide the cursor while typing/deleting. | | `cursorChar` | `string \| React.ReactNode` | `"\|"` | Character or node rendered as the cursor. | | `cursorClassName` | `string` | `"ml-1"` | Classes applied to the cursor element. | | `cursorAnimationVariants` | `{ initial: Variants["initial"]; animate: Variants["animate"] }` | blinking | Animation variants for the cursor. |

---
title: Introduction
description: A shadcn registry by Pranjal Mandavkar, shipping polished, ready-to-install components.
---

**prsui** is a [shadcn registry](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/registry) by [Pranjal Mandavkar](https://x.com/pr5dev) — a curated collection of polished, ready-to-install components you can drop into any React project.

## Add the Registry

First, add the prsui registry to your project:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest registry add @prsui=https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/{name}.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest registry add @prsui=https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/{name}.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest registry add @prsui=https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/{name}.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest registry add @prsui=https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/{name}.json"
/>

## Components

display.text:
---
title: Introduction
description: A shadcn registry by Pranjal Mandavkar, shipping polished, ready-to-install components.
---

**prsui** is a [shadcn registry](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/registry) by [Pranjal Mandavkar](https://x.com/pr5dev) — a curated collection of polished, ready-to-install components you can drop into any React project.

## Add the Registry

First, add the prsui registry to your project:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest registry add @prsui=https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/{name}.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest registry add @prsui=https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/{name}.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest registry add @prsui=https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/{name}.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest registry add @prsui=https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/{name}.json"
/>

## Components

The registry currently ships:

- **[Smooth Input](/docs/components/smooth-input)** - A smooth input component with a caret animation.

## FAQ

<Accordion type="multiple">

<AccordionItem value="faq-1">
<AccordionTrigger>
Why copy/paste instead of npm packages?
</AccordionTrigger>
<AccordionContent>

The shadcn philosophy gives you **ownership** of the code. You can customize components to your exact needs without fighting library constraints.

</AccordionContent>
</AccordionItem>

<AccordionItem value="faq-2">
<AccordionTrigger>
Which frameworks are supported?
</AccordionTrigger>
<AccordionContent>

Any React framework: Next.js, Remix, Astro, Gatsby, Vite, etc.

</AccordionContent>
</AccordionItem>

<AccordionItem value="faq-3">
<AccordionTrigger>
Can I request a component?
</AccordionTrigger>
<AccordionContent>

Reach out to [Pranjal Mandavkar](https://x.com/pr5dev) with suggestions — more components are added over time.

</AccordionContent>
</AccordionItem>

</Accordion>

parts.text:
Now I have everything I need. Let me also check how the component is exported from the registry to understand the import pattern for the demo. The mouse-follow component exports `SimpleMouseFollow` and `SpringMouseFollow` as named exports.

Let me now:
1. Add the mouse-follow entry to registry.json
2. Create the demo file
3. Create the MDX docs file

input.content:
--- title: Mouse Follow description: Animated cursor-following component with spring physics and opacity transitions. --- import { MouseFollowPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/mouse-follow-demo"; ## Installation If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command: Otherwise, use the full registry URL: ## Usage After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder: ```tsx "use client"; import { SimpleMouseFollow, SpringMouseFollow } from "./mouse-follow"; export const MouseFollowPreview = () => (
); ``` ## Exports | Export | Description | | --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `SimpleMouseFollow` | Basic mouse-following dot with opacity fade in/out on pointer enter/leave. | | `SpringMouseFollow` | Spring-physics mouse-following dot with scale and opacity spring animation. | ## Spring Config The `SpringMouseFollow` uses the following spring configuration: | Property | Value | Description | | ---------- | ----- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | | `mass` | `0.1` | Lower mass = snappier motion; higher mass = lethargic. | | `damping` | `10` | Controls bounce — higher damping = less bounce. | | `stiffness`| `131` | Like a rubber band — higher stiffness = faster return. |
